CONTENT.Honours: BATE BorisovBelarusian Premier League champion: 2018Belarusian Cup winner: 2019–20
CONTENT.Club career: On 6 August 2020, the BFF banned Volkov from Belarusian football for 2 years for his involvement in the match fixing during his time in Naftan. The ban was later extended to all FIFA-approved competitions. In September 2021, the reminder 1 year of his suspension was indefinitely delayed by BFF and he was able to return on pitch in domestic competitions.On 20 January 2022, he joined Russian Premier League club Khimki on loan until the end of the 2021–22 season.
CONTENT.External links: Zakhar Volkov at SoccerwayZakhar Volkov – UEFA competition record (archive)Zakhar Volkov at FootballFacts.ru (in Russian)

SUMMARY: Zakhar Vladimirovich Volkov (Belarusian: Захар Уладзіміравіч Волкаў; Russian: Захар Владимирович Волков; born 12 August 1997) is a Belarusian professional footballer who plays as a centre-back for Russian club Khimki and for the Belarus national team.
